reasoning: The fetched FRED data shows the 2-year expected inflation rate (EXPINF2YR) as of 2026-01-01 is 2.449%. This is below the average of the previous 12 months (which is roughly 2.52%). Given the monthly volatility and the mean-reverting nature of this series, an increase from a relatively low point of 2.449% is more likely than a decrease. The statistical trend analysis yields a probability of 0.66 that the value will increase by 2026-02-17 (which corresponds to the expected release of the February 2026 data point). I am adopting this estimate.
